ORDER DENYING PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED BY RESPONDENT DIRECTOR OF THE DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JOHN RYAN'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITION DENIED. PETITIONER FAILS TO SUSTAIN HIS BURDEN OF CONVINCING THE COURT THE ADMINISTRATIVE FINDINGS ARE CONTRARY TO THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE. GANANIAN V. ZOLIN (1995) 33 C. A. 4TH 634, 640-641 APPLIES. WEAVING CONSTITUTES REASONABLE CAUSE TO STOP A DRIVER. OFFICER FRAASS' STATEMENTS COMPLY WITH EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 1280. DOCUMENTARY EVIDENCE SHOWS PETITIONER WAS PROPERLY INFORMED ABOUT THE IMPLIED CONSENT LAW. THE HEARING OFFICER ISN'T OBLIGED TO ACCEPT PETITIONER'S VERSION. EXCLUDING HIS "WITNESS" STATEMENTS WAS NOT ERROR; THEY WERE TECHNICALLY IRRELEVANT BECAUSE NONE OF THE PERSONS INTERVIEWED BY MR. KEATON WAS PRESENT AT THE SCENE OF THE INCIDENT (THE "STATEMENTS" WERE NOT AFFIDAVITS OF THOSE PERSONS) (EX. K, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES). MOREOVER, REFUSAL TO UNDERGO A CHEMICAL TEST SUPERVENES ANY SUCH TESTIMONY. THE ATTACK ON THE HEARING OFFICER'S COMPETENCY WHILE UNDERSTANDABLE LACKS CONVINCING MERIT, BECAUSE OF POLAND VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: QUENTIN L. KOPP, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-13-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing Petition For Writ Of Mandate;
Off Calendar Apr-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER JOHN RYAN'S Petition for Writ of Mandate. Appearances: Pamela J. Herzig, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 565-9600, appearing for Petitioner and moving party; Raymond W. Hamilton, Deputy Attorney General, State of California, representing Respondent, Dept. of Motor Vehicle and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Petition denied. Petitioner fails to sustain his burden of convincing the court the administrative findings are contrary to the weight of the evidence. Gananian v. Zolin (1995) 33 C. A. 4th 634, 640-641 applies. Weaving constitutes reasonable cause to stop a driver. Officer Fraass' statements comply with Evidence Code section 1280. Documentary evidence shows petitioner was properly informed about the implied consent law. The hearing officer isn't obliged to accept petitioner's version. Excluding his "witness" statements was not error; they were technically irrelevant because none of the persons interviewed by Mr. Keaton was present at the scene of the incident (the "statements" were not affidavits of those persons) (ex. K, Points and Authorities). Moreover, refusal to undergo a chemical test supervenes any such testimony. The attack on the hearing officer's competency while understandable lacks convincing merit, because of Poland vs. Department of Motor Vehicles. Order signed in open Court.(302/KOPP/PB)Judge: Quentin Kopp; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797
